message:
feat(robot,ensemble,serve,tui): offline session support, error tracking, and output collection from saved files

Robot ensemble commands now work with persisted state when the tmux session
is no longer live, enabling post-mortem analysis and synthesis of completed
ensemble runs:

- GetEnsemble, GetEnsembleStop, and GetEnsembleSynthesize check persisted
  state before checking tmux liveness, so offline sessions with saved state
  return their last known status instead of "session not found"
- GetEnsembleStop marks active-but-offline sessions as stopped in the state
  store, preventing stale "active" status from persisting indefinitely
- GetEnsembleSynthesize collects mode outputs from both live captures and
  saved output files on disk, with captured outputs taking precedence per
  mode when both sources exist

Ensemble output collection (internal/ensemble/collector.go):
- Add CollectFromSavedOutputs() which reads completed mode outputs from
  persisted output files referenced in assignment.OutputPath, with per-mode
  validation error tracking for missing/corrupt/empty files
- Add CollectModeOutputs() which merges live-captured and saved outputs,
  clearing validation errors for modes that have valid output from either
  source, and sorting final output by mode_id for deterministic ordering

Error tracking fix (internal/robot/ensemble.go):
- AssignmentError status was incorrectly incrementing Pending counter
  instead of the new Errors counter, making error counts invisible in
  robot output. Add Errors field to EnsembleSummary and fix the status
  switch to increment it correctly.
- Ensemble hints now surface error counts and suggest "review" action
  when modes have failed

Additional:
- Serve checkpoint handler improvements for cross-project resolution
- Branch handler test coverage
- TUI dashboard command improvements and test coverage
- Ensemble manager, state, and session store test improvements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
